content: If x = logabc, y = logbca, z = logcab, then the value of $${1 \over {1 + x}} + {1 \over {1 + y}} + {1 \over {1 + z}}$$ will be
explanation:
$$\because$$ $$x = {\log _a}bc$$

$$ \Rightarrow 1 + x = {\log _a}a + {\log _a}bc = {\log _a}abc$$

Similarly, $$1 + y = {\log _b}abc$$ and $$1 + z = {\log _c}abc$$

$$\therefore$$ $${1 \over {1 + x}} + {1 \over {1 + y}} + {1 \over {1 + z}} = {1 \over {{{\log }_a}abc}} + {1 \over {{{\log }_b}abc}} + {1 \over {{{\log }_c}abc}}$$

$$ = {\log _{abc}}a + {\log _{abc}}b + {\log _{abc}}c = {\log _{abc}}abc = 1$$

$${\log _3}x + {\log _3}y = 2 + {\log _3}2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _3}(xy) = {\log _3}({3^2}\,.\,2)$$ $$\therefore$$ xy = 18 ....... (i)

Also, $${\log _3}(x + y) = 2 \Rightarrow x + y = 9$$ ....... (ii)

On solving we get x = 6, y = 3 or x = 3, y = 6.

options.content: both a G.P. and a H.P.

explanation:
The sequence can be written as

$$\log a,\,(2\log a - \log b),\,(3\log a - 2\log b),\,\,.....$$

which are in A.P. having common difference as $$\log a - \log b$$.

content: If $$\left(x^2 \log _x 27\right) \cdot \log _9 x=x+4$$ then the value of $$x$$ is
If x satisfies the inequality $${\log _{25}}{x^2} + {({\log _5}x)^2} < 2$$, then x belongs to
explanation:
$${{\log {x^2}} \over {\log 25}} + {\left[ {{{\log x} \over {\log 5}}} \right]^2} < 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{2\log x} \over {2\log 5}} + {\left[ {{{\log x} \over {\log 5}}} \right]^2} - 2 < 0$$ [Here, $$y = {{\log x} \over {\log 5}}$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {y^2} + y - 2 < 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 2 < y < 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 2 < {{\log x} \over {\log 5}} < 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 2\log 5 < \log x < \log 5$$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over {25}} < x < 5$$, i.e. $$x \in \left( {{1 \over {25}},5} \right)$$

content: If 2 log(x + 1) $$ - $$ log(x<sup>2</sup> $$ - $$ 1) = log 2, then x =
explanation:
We have,

2 log (x + 1) $$ - $$ log(x2 $$ - $$ 1) = log 2

$$ \Rightarrow \log {{{{(x + 1)}^2}} \over {{x^2} - 1}} = \log \,2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {(x + 1)^2} = 2({x^2} - 1)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} + 2x + 1 = 2{x^2} - 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x - 3 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - 3)(x + 1) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = 3,\,x \ne - 1$$

content: If $$\log _2^6 + {1 \over {2x}} = {\log _2}\left( {{2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8} \right)$$, then the value of x are
explanation:
We have,

$$\log _2^6 + {1 \over {2x}} = {\log _2}\left( {{2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow 1 + \log _2^3 + {1 \over {2x}} = {\log _2}\left( {{2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{{2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8} \over 3} = {2^{1 + {1 \over {2x}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8 = {3.2.2^{{1 \over {2x}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {2^{{1 \over x}}} + 8 = {6.2^{{1 \over {2x}}}}$$

Let $$y = {2^{{1 \over {2x}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {y^2} + 8 = 6y$$

$$ \Rightarrow {y^2} - 6y + 8 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (y - 4)(y - 2) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = 4,2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {2^{{1 \over {2x}}}} = 4$$ and $${2^{{1 \over {2x}}}} = 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over {2x}} = 2$$ and $${1 \over {2x}} = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {1 \over 4},{1 \over 2}$$

content: If $$x + {\log _{10}}(1 + {2^x}) = x{\log _{10}}5 + {\log _{10}}6$$, then the value of x is
explanation:
We have,

$$x + {\log _{10}}(1 + {2^x}) = x{\log _{10}}5 + {\log _{10}}6$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _{10}}(1 + {2^x}) = x{\log _{10}}5 + {\log _{10}}6 - x$$

$$ = {\log _{10}}{5^x} + {\log _{10}} + - x{\log _{10}}10$$

$$ = {\log _{10}}({5^x}.6) - {\log _{10}}{10^x}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _{10}}(1 + {2^x}) = {\log _{10}}\left( {{{{5^x}.6} \over {{{10}^x}}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow 1 + {2^x} = {{{5^x}.6} \over {{{10}^x}}} = {6 \over {{2^x}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {2^x}(1 + {2^x}) = 6$$

$$ \Rightarrow t(1 + t) = 6$$ (let 2x = t)

$$ \Rightarrow {t^2} + t - 6 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (t + 3)(t - 2) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow t + 3 = 0$$

or t $$-$$ 2 = 0

$$ \Rightarrow t = 2$$ [$$ \because $$ neglect t = $$-$$3]

$$ \Rightarrow {2^x} = 2 \Rightarrow x = 1$$

We have,

$${\log _5}x.{\log _x}3x.{\log _{3x}}y = {\log _x}{x^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{\log x} \over {\log 5}} \times {{\log 3x} \over {\log x}} \times {{\log y} \over {\log 3x}} = 3{\log _x}y$$

[$$ \because $$ $${\log _b}a = {{\log a} \over {\log b}}$$ and $$\log {a^m} = m\log a$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {{\log y} \over {\log 5}} = 3$$ [$$ \because $$ loga a = 1]

$$ \Rightarrow \log y = 3\log 5$$

$$ \Rightarrow \log y = \log {5^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = {5^3} = 125$$

content: If $${\log _{0.3}}(x - 1) < {\log _{0.09}}(x - 1)$$, then x lies in the interval
explanation:
Given, $${\log _{0.3}}(x - 1) < {\log _{0.09}}(x - 1)$$

$$ \Rightarrow lo{g_{0.3}}(x - 1) < {\log _{{{(0.3)}^2}}}(x - 1)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _{0.3}}{(x - 1)^2} < {\log _{0.3}}(x - 1)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {(x - 1)^2} > x - 1$$ [$$ \because $$ $$0.3 < 1$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} + 1 - 2x - x + 1 > 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} - 3x + 2 > 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - 1)(x - 2) > 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow x < 1,x > 2 \Rightarrow x > 2$$ [Image]

$$ \therefore $$ $$(2,\infty )$$

Hence, x lies in the interval $$(2,\infty )$$.

Given, equation

$${x^{{{(\log 3x)}^2}}} - {9 \over 2}\log 3\,(x + 5) = 3\sqrt 3 $$

$$ \Rightarrow \left( {{{({{\log }_3}\,x)}^2} - {9 \over 2}{{\log }_3}x + 5} \right){\log _3}x = {3 \over 2}{\log _3}3$$

Put log3 x = t

$$ \therefore $$ $$\left( {{t^2} - {9 \over 2}t + 5} \right)t = {3 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2{t^2} - 9{t^2} + 10t - 3 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (t - 3)(t - 1)\left( {t - {1 \over 2}} \right) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow t = 3,\,1,\,{1 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _3}x = 3,\,1,\,{1 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {3^3},\,{3^1},\,{3^{{1 \over 2}}}$$
